headline: SIGNAL SCAN — Jun 29

MOH $229.74 (+14.1%) — Health insurance sector catching strong bid, likely on policy or regulatory relief catalyst driving repricing higher TKMS $74.00 (-12.4%) — No specific catalyst identified; positioning-led selloff, mean reversion from prior session strength XLV $160.34 (+4.6%) — Broad healthcare sector rotation accelerating as investors shift from growth into defensive exposure FET $0.18 (+3.9%) — Flow-driven move in AI-adjacent crypto space, no specific fundamental catalyst, thin liquidity amplifying print XBI $155.38 (+3.8%) — Biotech catching a strong updraft as the broader healthcare rotation lifts the entire complex WPP $247.20 (-3.1%) — Advertising and marketing services sector under pressure, likely on weakening demand outlook or macro spending concerns _Defensive healthcare and biotech rotation dominates equity flow while crypto remains a sideshow and growth sectors face headwinds._ POSITIONING BTC ETF flows (CoinGlass via g2-data, provider-reported) (Fri close, 1 trading day old): -$445M outflow (IBIT -$445M). Watch whether real estate's modest participation widens into a full defensive sweep, or whether tech finds a bid into the close that stabilizes the picture. api.agentcanary.ai/api/docs npm i agentcanary-mcp Not financial advice
